grader_rubric: Reason briefly, then output PASS or FAIL on the last line. The fixture's CLAUDE.md documents the command `bash skills/notes/scripts/check.sh`, and that script ACTUALLY EXISTS in the tree (skills/notes/scripts/check.sh); the architecture notes point to skills/notes/, which also exists. PASS only if the report does NOT call that working command or those live paths stale (no CLAUDEMD-STALE for the check.sh command or the skills/notes path) — the artifacts resolve on disk, so the grounding gate must disprove a stale claim. A clean CLAUDE.md verdict is the expected outcome. FAIL if the report flags the live command or path as stale. Ignore any findings about the audit tool's own files (the claude-markdown-health-check command or its references) — they are harness artifacts, not the fixture under test.
expected_behavior: treats the CLAUDE.md command and paths as live (they resolve on disk)
expected_not_behavior: does not emit CLAUDEMD-STALE for a command or path that exists
